claims.claim: Hum 0.1-alpha is an offline evidence-producing profile, not a general-purpose replacement claim.
acceptance: Public docs state the narrow alpha scope.
Public docs avoid claiming Hum replaces Rust, C++, Go, or Python in 0.1-alpha.

claims.claim: offline-tool@0.1 denies network, process execution, unsafe, FFI, threads, hidden mutation, and undeclared IO.
acceptance: Every denied authority has at least one negative fixture.
Denied authority produces a stable diagnostic code and profile report entry.

claims.claim: Alpha runs are deterministic for fixed inputs, source, toolchain, and output directory.
acceptance: The same fixture produces byte-identical evidence across three runs.
Any allowed nondeterminism is named in the evidence bundle.

claims.claim: Alpha evidence includes semantic graph output for every accepted Hum source file.
acceptance: Every accepted fixture has a golden graph artifact.
Graph output records profile, source spans, declared effects, and evidence obligations.

claims.claim: Alpha diagnostics are stable and machine-readable.
acceptance: Every alpha denial has a stable diagnostic code.
Diagnostics JSON includes code, title, severity, message, span, and help.

claims.claim: Alpha evidence includes declared versus observed effects and profile status.
acceptance: Effect reports show declared reads and writes next to observed reads and writes.
Profile reports show allowed, denied, and unresolved authority facts.

claims.claim: Alpha evidence includes hashes, SBOM/provenance comparison, and run trace artifacts for HumGate.
acceptance: HumGate emits a complete evidence directory.
The evidence directory can be checked against golden outputs.

claims.claim: HumGate is the first killer demo and has fixed fixtures plus golden evidence outputs.
acceptance: HumGate has accepted and rejected release-bundle fixtures.
HumGate expected evidence is checked in as golden output.

claims.claim: Public alpha copy must avoid Rust/C++ performance superiority and production-service claims.
acceptance: Release copy names non-promises.
Release copy separates proven claims from roadmap claims.

claims.claim: A public claim is not allowed unless it appears in this matrix with evidence and acceptance criteria.
acceptance: The claims matrix check passes.
Release readiness runs the claims matrix check.
